By integrating belief theory and subjective logic into deep learning models, the study seeks to improve the robustness of medical image analysis.

The performance of the EDL model will be evaluated using metrics such as the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC) and Cohen's Kappa score. The experimental design will involve comparing the EDL model with traditional deep learning models to assess its effectiveness in mitigating uncertainty and improving robustness. The results will provide insights into the potential of EDL in medical image analysis and its ability to enhance the reliability of diagnostic decisions.

results.experimental_blueprint: The proposed method involves implementing a Private Federated Learning framework that incorporates domain adaptation using a multi-task learning approach on the CIFAR-10 dataset. The experimental setup will allow for the comparison of model performance with and without domain adaptation, providing insights into the effectiveness of this approach in enhancing model accuracy in Private Federated Learning settings. Furthermore, the study will examine how different domain adaptation techniques impact model performance across heterogeneous client data.

results.research_direction: This research aims to investigate the potential of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) in improving the performance of Federated Learning (FL) models on non-Independent and Identically Distributed (non-IID) data, which is a significant challenge in FL. By leveraging GANs, the study seeks to develop more robust and generalizable FL models.
results.experimental_blueprint: The proposed method involves training a GAN using the Federated Averaging algorithm to generate synthetic data samples that can augment the training data of local models, thereby reducing the impact of non-IID data. The CIFAR-10 dataset will be used to evaluate the performance of the proposed approach, with test accuracy serving as the primary evaluation metric. The experimental setup will consist of multiple clients with non-IID data distributions, and the GAN-generated synthetic data will be used to supplement the local training data. The performance of the proposed approach will be compared to traditional FL methods to demonstrate its efficacy in improving test accuracy on non-IID data.
